title: Business Process Impact ‚Äî Incidents by Critical Application

description: Aggregates security incidents by business-critical application to inform risk assessment prioritization under Identify.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST CSF compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

grandmaExplanation: We watch for aggregates security incidents by business-critical application to inform risk assessment prioritization under identify. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `notable`; `app_criticality_lookup.csv`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Populate dest-to-app mapping; (2) refresh after architecture changes; (3) present in risk workshop; (4) tie to BIA refresh cycle.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
search `notable` earliest=-90d
| lookup app_criticality_lookup.csv dest OUTPUT criticality
| stats count by criticality, dest
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Business Process Impact ‚Äî Incidents by Critical Application (ID.RA-01)** ‚Äî Aggregates security incidents by business-critical application to inform risk assessment prioritization under Identify.

Documented **Data sources**: `notable`; `app_criticality_lookup.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
‚Ä¢ Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by criticality, dest** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart, Table
